perturbation.original_text: To hold in confidence any and a ll technical or b usiness informa tion about the company’s product which is disclosed, or ma de available to you directly o r indirectly, or is information you otherwise receive incident t o your participation in this di scussion
perturbation.changed_text: To protect any and a ll technical or b usiness informa tion about the company’s product which is disclosed, or ma de available to you directly o r indirectly, or is information you otherwise receive incident t o your participation in this di scussion
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'hold in confidence' with 'protect' changes the obligation's legal weight. 'Hold in confidence' implies a fiduciary duty, while 'protect' is more generic. This weakens the NDA's enforceability since the specific duty is now vague. This weakens the Confidential Information terms since its now vague
perturbation.contradicted_text: That you, shall at all times hold in trust, keep confidential a nd not disclose to any third party or make any use of the Confident ial Information beyond those ac tivities that are part of the Focus Group.
perturbation.justification: The original text used "hold in confidence," which implies a fiduciary duty. The changed text weakens this by using "protect," a more generic term. The contradicted text states, "shall at all times hold in trust, keep confidential." The changed text weakens the original, stronger, more specific obligation to 'hold in trust' which is incompatible with 'protect' and introduces a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: That any ideas, patentable or not patentable, or suggestions co ntributed by you during the discussion, as well as any ideas , developments, or inventions c onceived by you or others participating in the Focus Group, shall be the property of the company sponsoring this research study in an y manner it sees fit.
perturbation.changed_text: That any ideas, patentable or not patentable, or suggestions co ntributed by you during the discussion, as well as any ideas , developments, or inventions c onceived by you or others participating in the Focus Group, shall be licensed to the company sponsoring this research study in an y manner it sees fit.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall be the property of' to 'shall be licensed to' fundamentally alters ownership rights. 'Property of' grants full ownership, while 'licensed to' grants usage rights. This creates ambiguity about who actually owns the ideas generated and the extent of the company's rights. This contradicts the 'property of' term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: That any ideas, patentable or not patentable, or suggestions co ntributed by you during the discussion, as well as any ideas , developments, or inventions c onceived by you or others participating in the Focus Group, shall be licensed to the company sponsoring this research study in an y manner it sees fit.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the ideas 'shall be the property of' the company, indicating full ownership. The changed text states the ideas 'shall be licensed to' the company, indicating usage rights but not necessarily ownership. The contradicted text repeats the 'shall be licensed to' phrase. The change from 'property of' to 'licensed to', directly contradicts the original intent of full ownership established previously, and creates a contradiction with the contradicted text. The Perturbation Type given is 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction', which indicates the change introduces conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: The products shown to you, described to you, and/or used by you are not available for sale and no offer for sale is being made to you.
perturbation.changed_text: The products shown to you, described to you, and/or used by you may be available for demonstration and an offer for demonstration purposes is being made to you.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'not available for sale and no offer for sale is being made' to 'may be available for demonstration and an offer for demonstration purposes is being made' creates an in-text contradiction. It implies the products are available and are being offered for something, which directly conflicts with the original statement. This makes the restriction on use questionable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The products shown to you, described to you, and/or used by you may be available for demonstration and an offer for demonstration purposes is being made to you.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that the products are 'not available for sale and no offer for sale is being made'. The changed text replaces this with 'may be available for demonstration and an offer for demonstration purposes is being made'. This introduces a direct contradiction because the products, initially not for sale, are now implied to be available for demonstration purposes, creating an inconsistency regarding the product's availability and use. The change clearly conflicts with the contradicted text, which is an exact copy of the changed text, indicating that both sections within the same document now conflict with the original intent.

perturbation.original_text: PII shall mean a person’s identity or information that might re asonably allow identification of the person. I shall at all times hold in trust, keep confidenti al and not disclose to any third party or make any use of the identity or PII of any Respondent involved in the Focus Group.
perturbation.changed_text: PII may include a person’s identity or information that might re asonably allow identification of the person. I shall at all times take reasonable steps to keep confidential and not deliberately disclose to any third party or make any commercial use of the identity or PII of any Respondent involved in the Focus Group.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'PII shall mean' to 'PII may include', 'hold in trust, keep confidential and not disclose' to 'take reasonable steps to keep confidential and not deliberately disclose', and adding 'commercial use' drastically weakens PII protection. The obligation is now subjective and allows for accidental disclosure. This contradicts the intention of the NDA to protect PII.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Also included as confidential is any participants Personally Id entifiable Information (“PII”). PII may include a person’s identity or information that might re asonably allow identification of the person. I shall at all times take reasonable steps to keep confidential and not deliberately disclose to any third party or make any commercial use of the identity or PII of any Respondent involved in the Focus Group.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly weakens the PII protection compared to the original text by changing "shall mean" to "may include", "hold in trust, keep confidential and not disclose" to "take reasonable steps to keep confidential and not deliberately disclose", and adding "commercial use". This weakening of the obligation creates a strong contradiction within the document, making it unclear which standard of protection should be applied. The contradiction explanation also confirms that it "contradicts the intention of the NDA to protect PII" and that it "allows for accidental disclosure", causing uncertainty and a strong contradiction. The contradicted text uses the same modified language as the changed text, but this does not negate the fact that the initial change created a contradiction.
